meta.resumePath: LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/in/jeffrey-lam-3b542716 (public profile gated); prior verified extraction ServiceNow-Jeffrey-Lam-Resume-Match-Report.md; no PDF in ./assets/docs/

overallMatch.summary: Solid fit for strategic APAC GTM leadership: 15+ years enterprise SaaS, Workday Regional Sales Director scope, strong quota history, and Hong Kong / Greater China credibility. Main gaps versus the VP/Sr Director GTM Operations JD are explicit partnership-program ownership and demonstrated orchestration of Sales, CS, BDRs, and alliances as one regional system—address with STAR stories, alliance thesis, and operating cadence beyond quota-carrying sales.

granularScores.rationale: Strong consultative enterprise selling and Workday/Oracle HCM depth; less explicit on alliance P&L, partner enablement, and CS/BDR operating systems.
15+ years SaaS; RSD at Workday; complex enterprise cycles; top performer—strong vs proven GTM; broaden evidence beyond sales-only leadership.
Bachelor of Accounting (Curtin)—credible for financial and operational discussions at VP level.
Hong Kong SAR; English, Cantonese, Mandarin; Greater China enterprise footprint—strong APAC anchor.
Multi-vendor career (Infor, Oracle, Microsoft, Workday); IC to RSD; complex cycles—supports matrix GTM narratives.
VP/Sr Director regional GTM is a credible step; partnership ecosystem depth is the stretch dimension to emphasize.
Senior regional GTM packages at global SaaS firms are typically competitive; align OTE, equity, and scope with recruiter.

jobSpecificScores.rationale: Strong sales strategy and territory experience; show integrated GTM playbooks and cross-functional OKRs beyond quota.
Primary gap for this JD—surface channel, SI, co-sell, or strategic alliance outcomes explicitly.
RSD implies people leadership; dotted-line to CS, BDR, partnerships needs concrete examples (cadence, QBRs, councils).
Profile is sales-weighted; add renewal, expansion, and success collaboration tied to CLV.
Deep Greater China enterprise experience; articulate multi-hub APAC expansion and partner strategy.
C-level selling (e.g., CHRO/CFO patterns); map to regional face and feedback loops to global product/marketing.
Workday, Oracle, Microsoft—tier-1 enterprise SaaS; EOR category can be ramped quickly.
JD stresses building programs from scratch—weakest explicit signal; mitigate with frameworks and any program work.

jobRequirements.requirement: Proven GTM leadership in Sales, Partnerships, or BD (tech/SaaS preferred)
jobRequirements.evidence: 15+ years enterprise SaaS; Workday Regional Sales Director; sustained high attainment.
RSD scope; cross-functional enterprise deals; prepare explicit dotted-line rituals.

jobRequirements.requirement: Partnership specialist: building partnership programs from the ground up
jobRequirements.evidence: Direct sales emphasis in prior extraction; alliance stories need amplification.
Hong Kong base; Greater China enterprise logos; trilingual.
C-suite enterprise selling; complex stakeholder maps.

jobRequirements.requirement: Lead regional GTM: Sales, BDR, CS aligned on targets and playbooks
jobRequirements.evidence: Sales leadership strong; BDR/CS orchestration less documented—prepare operating model.

jobRequirements.requirement: Scale strategic partnerships as force multiplier
jobRequirements.evidence: Co-sell/ecosystem examples to be made explicit in materials and interviews.

jobRequirements.requirement: Bridge local market needs and global strategy; CS and Sales integrated for CLV
jobRequirements.evidence: Prepare CLV and expansion stories with CS alignment.

strengths: 15+ years enterprise SaaS GTM and Workday Regional Sales Director scope.
Sustained high quota attainment and top-performer recognition patterns.
Hong Kong / Greater China anchor with English, Cantonese, and Mandarin.
Workday and Oracle HCM fluency adjacent to employer, HR, and workforce buyers.
C-level enterprise selling experience transferable to regional spokesperson expectations.

gaps.gap: Partnership-program ownership and alliance outcomes not explicit on resume.
gaps.recommendation: Add metrics: partners enabled, co-sell pipeline, SI alliances, referral growth, and a one-page APAC alliance thesis.

gaps.gap: Sales-weighted profile vs integrated GTM (CS, BDR, partnerships).
gaps.recommendation: Prepare a regional operating cadence: weekly pipeline, partner pipeline, health scores, and CS interlock examples.

gaps.gap: G-P product fluency (EOR, Contractor, Gia) not yet demonstrated.
gaps.recommendation: Study G-P materials; tie Gia compliance AI to partner differentiation and retention.

interviewPrepFocus: 90-day APAC GTM plan: partners, Sales–CS rituals, BDR sourcing mix, KPIs.
Partnership ecosystem: SI, referral, tech alliances; country prioritization.
Matrix leadership without direct reports: cadence, data, executive sponsorship.
Gia and compliance narrative for APAC multi-country hiring complexity.
Regional advocacy: external presence and feedback to global product/marketing.
actionableRecommendations: Update LinkedIn with a Partnerships & ecosystem subsection (co-sell $, partners enabled, alliance wins).
In recruiter screen, ask about current APAC partner structure and 2026 priorities.
Prepare three STAR stories: matrix alignment, partnership win, expansion/retention with CS.
Map G-P Gia to APAC compliance and speed-to-hire use cases.
Confirm reporting line, VP vs Sr Director scope, and compensation band.

highlights.executiveSummary:
## Executive Summary

**G-P** (Globalization Partners) is a leading **global employment platform** and top-tier **Employer of Record (EOR)** provider. Organizations use G-P to **hire, onboard, manage, and pay** people in **180+ countries** without establishing local entities.

### Products & differentiation

- **EOR** — Core compliant employment and payroll at scale.
- **G-P Gia™** — Agentic **AI for global HR compliance** (documents, policies, compliance intelligence) built on a **proprietary knowledge base** (100,000+ vetted articles, 1,500+ government sources).
- **G-P Contractor** — Contractor engagement and payments with ongoing product investment.

### APAC & GTM context

- Localized **Gia** pages (e.g. **China** `/cn/gia/`) signal **APAC** investment and **compliance-led** storytelling.
- The **VP/Sr Director GTM Operations (APAC)** role fits a **partner-heavy**, **matrix** GTM: **Sales**, **Partnerships**, **CS**, and **BDRs** must **cohesively** execute **regional** growth.

### Leadership & verification

- Confirm **named CEO** and **APAC** leadership on **g-p.com** and LinkedIn before interviews.
- Culture emphasizes **trust**, **compliance innovation**, and **global scale**—use **Gia** as the **AI** hook in partner and customer conversations.
highlights.leadership: Verify current CEO and APAC regional leaders on official G-P / g-p.com channels and LinkedIn; G-P emphasizes global scale with local compliance expertise.
highlights.products: EOR (core), G-P Gia (agentic HR compliance AI), G-P Contractor; Gia supported by proprietary knowledge base and G-P-verified sources.
highlights.culture: Customer-centric global employment platform; innovation and compliance trust; matrix-friendly partner and direct motions.
highlights.recommendation: Anchor candidate stories on partnership-led growth, Sales–CS–BDR alignment, and Gia-enabled differentiation for APAC.
